title: Quay x Masonry: a creative supercomputer for your brand
meta: Vertical 9:16 reel, ~46s. Featured brand: Quay Australia. Inspired by Tim Koda's AI creative-agent reel (instagram.com/p/DZbFxeDsH8H).
concept: A fast-cut hype reel that reframes Masonry as a creative supercomputer that holds a brand's entire identity and ships campaigns on its own. We open inside an AI agent reviewing a Quay campaign and catching what human review missed, build through everything it can generate and the consistency it guards, then pay it off with a surreal festival scene where the campaign is already live. Mirrors the inspiration's beat order, single-word on-screen punches, and length, with fully original copy.
audio: Driving electronic hype track at the same fast-cut tempo as the inspiration. One confident voiceover carries the claims; the on-screen text punches a single key word each beat, snapped to the cuts. If produced audio-only, drop the VO and let the OST plus the track carry the story.

beats.vo: I handed my whole campaign to one AI agent and watched it catch what three rounds of review missed.
beats.visual: Terminal-style Masonry chat reviewing a Quay campaign clip frame by frame, a scan readout ticking, flagged issues lighting up on the right.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 0 to 2s, the AI agent reviewing a video in a terminal and detecting anomalies.

beats.vo: This is the thing that changed how we make content.
beats.visual: Quick whip into a glossy reveal card over the chat, big word stamped on screen, surreal AI-generated backdrop behind it.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 3 to 4s, the revolutionized and feature reveal cards.

beats.vo: It holds your entire brand at once. Every shot, every color, every line you have ever shipped.
beats.visual: Counter rolling up to a huge number, a wall of past Quay assets loading into the agent's memory.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 6 to 7s, the token counter and capacity claim.

beats.vo: So everything it makes stays on brand without you babysitting it.
beats.visual: Dashboard view, brand consistency meters at full, a row of generated Quay frames all matching.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 8 to 9s, the stay and inside dashboard cards.

beats.vo: Masonry. A creative supercomputer for your brand.
beats.visual: Product name slams on over the chat composer, model selector reading Masonry, tagline underneath.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 10 to 13s, the supercomputer for creators reveal and the chat bar.

beats.vo: Tell it what you need. Ads, product shots, avatars, full videos.
beats.visual: Grid of outputs spawning at once: a Quay ad, a clean eyewear product shot, a model avatar, a short clip.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 14 to 16s, the what can it do menu and generation grid.

beats.vo: It generates the whole set in seconds, on brand and ready to post.
beats.visual: Tiles flipping from placeholder to finished, web banners and vertical video filling in.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 16 to 18s, the web and videos output beats.

beats.vo: And it catches the morphing and the warps before they ever reach your feed.
beats.visual: Anomaly inspector on a generated clip, a frame marked, the fix applied and the warp gone.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 19 to 22s, the morphing inconsistencies and anomaly list.

beats.vo: Give it a new skill and its range just keeps growing.
beats.visual: Skill cards snapping into place, a capacity bar pushing to max.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 22 to 25s, the skills, capacity and max cards.

beats.vo: It runs like a full creative team you direct from one chat.
beats.visual: The agent spinning up parallel jobs on its own, a checklist of campaign tasks completing.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 26 to 30s, the autonomous and building categories beats.

beats.vo: Every tool you already use is plugged straight in.
beats.visual: Connectors panel sliding up, app tiles linking into the chat.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 30 to 34s, the supercomputer connectors reveal.

beats.vo: Instagram, Notion, Slack, your whole stack, all connected.
beats.visual: Scrolling list of connected apps, each lighting up as it links.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 35 to 39s, the connectors list of apps.

beats.vo: So the campaign ships itself while you are out living it.
beats.visual: Two creators in Quay sunglasses at a sunlit festival, surreal floating shapes around them, the campaign already live.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 40 to 43s, the celebrating creators payoff.

beats.vo: Masonry. Your brand's creative DNA, in one place.
beats.visual: Festival wide shot dissolving to a clean Quay logo lockup with the Masonry mark and a comment to unlock CTA.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 44 to 45s, the DNA stamp and brand sign-off.

notes: Quay Australia is a stand-in demo brand (a believable D2C eyewear pick), not a signed client. All media is generated and assembled with Masonry, positioned as the creative supercomputer. No media has been generated yet; generation is gated on separate approval per Rule 16. Copy is original and mirrors the inspiration's structure only, not its lines.
refsummary: The original (Tim Koda) frames an AI creative agent as a supercomputer that holds a brand's whole identity and ships campaigns on its own, fast single-word cards over a slick UI: scanning, revolutionized, always on brand, autonomous, connectors. Watch it for the rapid single-word punch cards and the agent-supercomputer framing; our version makes it Masonry x Quay.
story: Same supercomputer-agent framing, reframed to Masonry with Quay Australia as the demo brand. Arc: HOOK, inside a Masonry agent scanning a Quay campaign and catching what human review missed. THE CLAIM, your whole brand, remembered, always on brand. WHAT IT MAKES, generated visuals and video, no morphing, new skills, autonomous, every tool connected, Instagram, Notion, Slack. PAYOFF, it ships itself, Quay x Masonry. Keep the single-word on-screen punches and fast cuts. The viewer should see Masonry as a creative supercomputer that runs a brand's campaigns end to end.
takeaway: Masonry is a creative supercomputer that holds your entire brand, generates on-brand campaigns, guards consistency, connects to your tools and effectively ships the work itself.
